title: Biotech violently bounces: XBI +3.88%, IBB +2.27%, ARKG +5.05% on Wednesday
description: After a multi-week selloff, longevity-adjacent biotech ETFs ripped Wednesday — XBI +3.88%, IBB +2.27%, SBIO +3.75%, IDNA +2.92%, BBH +0.96%, and notably ARKG +5.05%. The bid suggests sentiment turn ahead of expected ASCO and BIO catalysts, and pulls geroscience public-market valuations off recent lows.

title: Aging-US editorial: healthspan now requires layered public-health + longevity-medicine integration
description: A new Aging-US editorial argues that healthy longevity will depend on the coordinated integration of multiple intervention layers — public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine — rather than any single magic bullet. This signals the field's formal pivot from biomarker discovery to translational policy and clinical-pathway design.

title: NG101 first to show structural CNS rescue in chronic human spinal cord injury
description: NervGen's NG101 Phase 2b spinal cord injury data show imaging biomarkers consistent with slowed degeneration and possible fiber sprouting — the first human evidence that a small molecule may halt or partially reverse trauma-driven CNS structural decline. The result has read-throughs to neurodegenerative aging programs that share regenerative substrate biology.

title: Sirolimus-style rapamycin trials now multi-site: UT San Antonio + Univ. of Arizona Phase 3
description: Following the NIA-backed UT San Antonio dose-finding launch, U. of Arizona Coit College of Pharmacy will run a 2-year double-blind Phase 3 of low-dose rapamycin in adults 65+ funded by $12M philanthropic gift — measuring frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging. Rapamycin now has its biggest, most rigorous Western trial footprint to date.

title: Klemera-Doubal bio-age clock responds to 4-week diet — clock methodology matters
description: Reanalysis of the Sydney study shows the deltaAge signal is detectable specifically with Klemera-Doubal Method clocks, not Horvath/PhenoAge. This refines protocol design: short-window diet trials need simple-marker KDM scoring, not epigenetic panels, to detect intervention response.

title: Extending Healthspan Through Public Health and Longevity Medicine
description: New editorial: healthy longevity will depend on coordinated integration of multiple intervention layers — public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine.

title: NG101 and Nerve Regeneration in Human Spinal Cord Injury
description: In a Phase 2b SCI trial, NG101 imaging biomarkers suggested slowed trauma-induced macro- and microstructural degeneration and possible fiber sprouting.

title: Sirtuin 1 and Exercise
description: Researchers connect SIRT1 expression to improved metabolism following exercise, while noting causal attribution is hard because exercise changes many systems at once.

title: Simple-Marker Clocks and Short-Term Diet Change
description: A 4-week dietary intervention in 104 adults 65-75 showed Klemera-Doubal Method deltaAge responded, especially in the omnivorous/high-carbohydrate group — KDM may outperform epigenetic clocks for short-window trials.

title: Aging the Gut Microbiome and Intestinal Immune Function
description: In aged mice, intestinal immune aging was associated with epithelial barrier dysfunction, inflammatory bacterial enrichment, impaired M cell function, and dysregulated mucosal immunity.

title: TREM2 and Retinal Aging in Macular Degeneration
description: Review summarizes evidence that retinal myeloid cells and TREM2-associated programs may influence both dry and neovascular age-related macular degeneration.

title: Cellular Senescence, Heterogeneity, and Precision Senolytics
description: Review argues senescent cells are functionally heterogeneous; future senolytics may need to be precise, non-toxic clearance tools targeted at harmful subpopulations.

title: Platelets, HuR, and Systemic Aging
description: Aged-mouse data show platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors rise systemically; platelet-specific HuR deletion reduced inflammation and delayed systemic aging.

title: Immune Priming and Improved Wound Healing in Aged Skin
description: A short pre-injury pulse of LPS accelerated wound repair in aged mice by improving immune cell activity, keratinocyte responses, and barrier restoration.

title: Melanoma and Age-Dependent Gamma Delta T Cell Responses
description: Older-mouse melanoma models show middle-aged animals had fewer protective gamma delta T cells and more metastatic spread than young or very old mice.

title: Physical Activity and DNA Methylation Clocks
description: Systematic review and meta-analysis: higher physical activity is associated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration, but not Hannum or PhenoAge.

title: Sociality and Longevity in Mammals
description: Comparative analysis of 1,436 mammal species found group-living and pair-living species generally had longer lifespans than solitary species after controlling for body mass.

title: Integrator, RNA Processing, and Lifespan in C. elegans
description: Adult depletion of most Integrator subunits extended lifespan and healthspan in C. elegans via defective pre-mRNA processing, altered siRNAs, and mitochondrial dysfunction.

title: U of Arizona launches $12M rapamycin Phase 3 trial in older adults
description: R. Ken Coit College of Pharmacy 2-year randomized double-blind low-dose rapamycin trial in 65+ adults measuring frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging marker, with one additional year of follow-up.

title: Large rapamycin trial launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: NIA-funded multi-phase rapamycin/everolimus study in healthy adults 65-90 launches at UT San Antonio, structured to define optimal anti-aging dose ahead of a 6-month placebo-controlled Phase trial in 84 participants.

title: Methylene Blue Protects Hair Follicle Stem Cells from GLP-1 Metabolic Stress
description: University of Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death.

title: Precision Anti-Aging Strategies Aim to Target Harmful Senescent Cells
description: New Volume 18 review argues senescent cells include essential subsets in embryonic development, wound healing and tissue homeostasis — pushing the field toward precision clearance of harmful subpopulations.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal antibody hit primary endpoint in MIRANDA Phase 3 COPD trial, validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for one of the most common diseases of aging.

title: Insilico forms Longevity Board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico Medicine stands up dedicated Longevity Board to oversee its AI-driven aging research program, formalizing its commitment to geroscience translation alongside the recent $2.75B Eli Lilly deal.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, its NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program, supporting further development in cardiometabolic and inflammaging indications.

title: Seragon SRN-901 extends median lifespan in mice
description: Seragon Biosciences reports SRN-901 extended median remaining lifespan 33% in 18-month-old mice — a notable late-life rejuvenation signal building on April Rejuvenation Roundup coverage.

title: Inflammation's statin moment edges closer
description: Feature on the maturation of broad anti-inflammatory geroprotectors and how the field is positioning IL-6 / NLRP3 / IL-33 modulators as the cardiovascular-statin analogue for inflammaging.

title: Mesoblast invests in smarter cell therapies with new CAR tech deal
description: Mesoblast deepens its position in next-generation cell therapy with a CAR technology partnership, adding to the cell-based aging-therapeutic platform pipeline.

title: Are global brands and employers longevity-ready?
description: Ecosystem feature on whether global consumer brands and employers are operationally ready to integrate longevity products and protocols for aging workforces and consumers.

title: Diet rewires biological age in 4 weeks — Sydney Aging Cell study
description: University of Sydney trial in adults 65-75 found 4 weeks of lower-fat or more plant-based protein diets reduced estimated biological age via KDM clocks; strongest signal from the lower-fat, higher-carb arm.
